title: Exotheca abyssinica
intro: Exotheca abyssinica, also known as African bitter leaf, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. Traditionally, Exotheca abyssinica has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including reducing fever, treating malaria, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Exotheca abyssinica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Exotheca abyssinica is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in water to relieve coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory issues, as well as being used as an ingredient in traditional remedies for treating fever and malaria.
